chunks.text: Vern Paxson is Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley EECS. His contact information includes office at 737 Soda Hall with phone 3-4209, and 630 International Computer Science Institute with phone 666-2882. Email is vern@berkeley.edu. Office hours are by appointment via Zoom. Research areas are Security (SEC) and Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT). Research center affiliation is International Computer Science Institute (ICSI).

Selected publications include: D. Fifield et al., 'Blocking-resistant communication through domain fronting' in Privacy Enhancing Technologies Symposium 2015. K. Thomas et al., 'Ad Injection at Scale: Assessing Deceptive Advertisement Modifications' in IEEE Security and Privacy 2015. P. Pearce et al., 'Characterizing Large-Scale Click Fraud in ZeroAccess' in ACM CCS 2014. S. Khattak et al., 'A Look at the Consequences of Internet Censorship Through an ISP Lens' in ACM SIGCOMM IMC 2014. Z. Durumeric et al., 'The Matter of Heartbleed' in ACM IMC 2014.

Additional selected publications: A. Kapravelos et al., 'Hulk: eliciting malicious behavior in browser extensions' in USENIX Security 2014. W. R. Marczak et al., 'When Governments Hack Opponents: A Look at Actors and Technology' in USENIX Security 2014. K. Thomas et al., 'Consequences of Connectivity: Characterizing Account Hijacking on Twitter' in ACM CCS 2014. S. Khattak et al., 'Towards Illuminating a Censorship Monitor’s Model to Facilitate Evasion' in USENIX FOCI 2013. M. Javed and V. Paxson, 'Detecting stealthy, distributed SSH brute-forcing' in ACM CCS 2013.

Further publications: V. Paxson et al., 'Practical Comprehensive Bounds on Surreptitious Communication over DNS' in USENIX Security 2013. K. Thomas et al., 'Trafficking Fraudulent Accounts: The Role of the Underground Market in Twitter Spam and Abuse' in USENIX Security 2013. M. Dhawan et al., 'Fathom: A browser-based network measurement platform' in ACM IMC 2012. C. Rossow et al., 'Prudent Practices for Designing Malware Experiments: Status Quo and Outlook' in IEEE Security and Privacy 2012. L. Martignoni et al., 'Cloud Terminal: Secure Access to Sensitive Applications from Untrusted Systems' in USENIX ATC 2012.

Older publications include: K. Thomas et al., 'Adapting social spam infrastructure for political censorship' in USENIX LEET 2012. K. Levchenko et al., 'Click Trajectories: End-to-End Analysis of the Spam Value Chain' in IEEE SP 2011. C. Kanich et al., 'Show Me the Money: Characterizing Spam-advertised Revenue' in USENIX Security 2011. J. Caballero et al., 'Measuring Pay-Per-Install: The Commoditization of Malware Distribution' in USENIX Security 2011. K. Thomas et al., 'Design and evaluation of a real-time URL spam filtering service' in IEEE SP 2011.

Selected honors and awards: USENIX Test of Time Award 2022, IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy Test of Time Award 2020, Internet Defense Prize 2017, Signatures Innovation Fellow 2016, IEEE Internet Award 2015, Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science 2013, ACM SIGCOMM Award 2011, Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science 2011, Grace Murray Hopper Award 2007, ACM Fellow 2006, ACM SIGCOMM Test of Time Paper Award 2006, IEEE ComSoc William R. Bennett Prize 2002 and 1998.
